tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueBatchCreatePartition.batchCreatePartition
tools.description: Creates one or more partitions in a batch operation.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueBatchDeleteConnection.batchDeleteConnection
tools.description: Deletes a list of connection definitions from the Data Catalog.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueBatchDeletePartition.batchDeletePartition
tools.description: Deletes one or more partitions in a batch operation.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueBatchDeleteTable.batchDeleteTable
tools.description:
Deletes multiple tables at once.

After completing this operation, you no longer have access to the table versions and partitions that belong to the deleted table. Glue deletes these "orphaned" resources asynchronously in a timely manner, at the discretion of the service.

To ensure the immediate deletion of all related resources, before calling BatchDeleteTable, use DeleteTableVersion or BatchDeleteTableVersion, and DeletePartition or BatchDeletePartition, to delete any resources that belong to the table.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueBatchDeleteTableVersion.batchDeleteTableVersion
tools.description: Deletes a specified batch of versions of a table.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueBatchGetBlueprints.batchGetBlueprints
tools.description: Retrieves information about a list of blueprints.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueBatchGetCrawlers.batchGetCrawlers
tools.description: Returns a list of resource metadata for a given list of crawler names. After calling the ListCrawlers operation, you can call this operation to access the data to which you have been granted permissions. This operation supports all IAM permissions, including permission conditions that uses tags.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueBatchGetCustomEntityTypes.batchGetCustomEntityTypes
tools.description: Retrieves the details for the custom patterns specified by a list of names.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueBatchGetDataQualityResult.batchGetDataQualityResult
tools.description: Retrieves a list of data quality results for the specified result IDs.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueBatchGetDevEndpoints.batchGetDevEndpoints
tools.description: Returns a list of resource metadata for a given list of development endpoint names. After calling the ListDevEndpoints operation, you can call this operation to access the data to which you have been granted permissions. This operation supports all IAM permissions, including permission conditions that uses tags.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueBatchGetJobs.batchGetJobs
tools.description: Returns a list of resource metadata for a given list of job names. After calling the ListJobs operation, you can call this operation to access the data to which you have been granted permissions. This operation supports all IAM permissions, including permission conditions that uses tags.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueBatchGetPartition.batchGetPartition
tools.description: Retrieves partitions in a batch request.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueBatchGetTriggers.batchGetTriggers
tools.description: Returns a list of resource metadata for a given list of trigger names. After calling the ListTriggers operation, you can call this operation to access the data to which you have been granted permissions. This operation supports all IAM permissions, including permission conditions that uses tags.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueBatchGetWorkflows.batchGetWorkflows
tools.description: Returns a list of resource metadata for a given list of workflow names. After calling the ListWorkflows operation, you can call this operation to access the data to which you have been granted permissions. This operation supports all IAM permissions, including permission conditions that uses tags.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueBatchStopJobRun.batchStopJobRun
tools.description: Stops one or more job runs for a specified job definition.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueBatchUpdatePartition.batchUpdatePartition
tools.description: Updates one or more partitions in a batch operation.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueCancelDataQualityRuleRecommendationRun.cancelDataQualityRuleRecommendationRun
tools.description: Cancels the specified recommendation run that was being used to generate rules.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueCancelDataQualityRulesetEvaluationRun.cancelDataQualityRulesetEvaluationRun
tools.description: Cancels a run where a ruleset is being evaluated against a data source.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueCancelMlTaskRun.cancelMlTaskRun
tools.description: Cancels (stops) a task run. Machine learning task runs are asynchronous tasks that Glue runs on your behalf as part of various machine learning workflows. You can cancel a machine learning task run at any time by calling CancelMLTaskRun with a task run's parent transform's TransformID and the task run's TaskRunId.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueCancelStatement.cancelStatement
tools.description: Cancels the statement.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueCheckSchemaVersionValidity.checkSchemaVersionValidity
tools.description: Validates the supplied schema. This call has no side effects, it simply validates using the supplied schema using DataFormat as the format. Since it does not take a schema set name, no compatibility checks are performed.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueCreateBlueprint.createBlueprint
tools.description: Registers a blueprint with Glue.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueCreateClassifier.createClassifier
tools.description: Creates a classifier in the user's account. This can be a GrokClassifier, an XMLClassifier, a JsonClassifier, or a CsvClassifier, depending on which field of the request is present.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueCreateConnection.createConnection
tools.description: Creates a connection definition in the Data Catalog.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueCreateCrawler.createCrawler
tools.description: Creates a new crawler with specified targets, role, configuration, and optional schedule. At least one crawl target must be specified, in the s3Targets field, the jdbcTargets field, or the DynamoDBTargets field.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueCreateCustomEntityType.createCustomEntityType
tools.description:
Creates a custom pattern that is used to detect sensitive data across the columns and rows of your structured data.

Each custom pattern you create specifies a regular expression and an optional list of context words. If no context words are passed only a regular expression is checked.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueCreateDatabase.createDatabase
tools.description: Creates a new database in a Data Catalog.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueCreateDataQualityRuleset.createDataQualityRuleset
tools.description:
Creates a data quality ruleset with DQDL rules applied to a specified Glue table.

You create the ruleset using the Data Quality Definition Language (DQDL). For more information, see the Glue developer guide.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueCreateDevEndpoint.createDevEndpoint
tools.description: Creates a new development endpoint.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueCreateJob.createJob
tools.description: Creates a new job definition.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueCreateMlTransform.createMlTransform
tools.description:
Creates an Glue machine learning transform. This operation creates the transform and all the necessary parameters to train it.

Call this operation as the first step in the process of using a machine learning transform (such as the FindMatches transform) for deduplicating data. You can provide an optional Description, in addition to the parameters that you want to use for your algorithm.

You must also specify certain parameters for the tasks that Glue runs on your behalf as part of learning from your data and creating a high-quality machine learning transform. These parameters include Role, and optionally, AllocatedCapacity, Timeout, and MaxRetries. For more information, see Jobs.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueCreatePartition.createPartition
tools.description: Creates a new partition.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueCreatePartitionIndex.createPartitionIndex
tools.description: Creates a specified partition index in an existing table.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueCreateRegistry.createRegistry
tools.description: Creates a new registry which may be used to hold a collection of schemas.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueCreateSchema.createSchema
tools.description:
Creates a new schema set and registers the schema definition. Returns an error if the schema set already exists without actually registering the version.

When the schema set is created, a version checkpoint will be set to the first version. Compatibility mode "DISABLED" restricts any additional schema versions from being added after the first schema version. For all other compatibility modes, validation of compatibility settings will be applied only from the second version onwards when the RegisterSchemaVersion API is used.

When this API is called without a RegistryId, this will create an entry for a "default-registry" in the registry database tables, if it is not already present.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueCreateScript.createScript
tools.description: Transforms a directed acyclic graph (DAG) into code.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueCreateSecurityConfiguration.createSecurityConfiguration
tools.description: Creates a new security configuration. A security configuration is a set of security properties that can be used by Glue. You can use a security configuration to encrypt data at rest. For information about using security configurations in Glue, see Encrypting Data Written by Crawlers, Jobs, and Development Endpoints.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueCreateSession.createSession
tools.description: Creates a new session.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueCreateTable.createTable
tools.description: Creates a new table definition in the Data Catalog.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueCreateTrigger.createTrigger
tools.description: Creates a new trigger.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueCreateUserDefinedFunction.createUserDefinedFunction
tools.description: Creates a new function definition in the Data Catalog.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueCreateWorkflow.createWorkflow
tools.description: Creates a new workflow.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueDeleteBlueprint.deleteBlueprint
tools.description: Deletes an existing blueprint.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueDeleteClassifier.deleteClassifier
tools.description: Removes a classifier from the Data Catalog.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueDeleteColumnStatisticsForPartition.deleteColumnStatisticsForPartition
tools.description:
Delete the partition column statistics of a column.

The Identity and Access Management (IAM) permission required for this operation is DeletePartition.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueDeleteColumnStatisticsForTable.deleteColumnStatisticsForTable
tools.description:
Retrieves table statistics of columns.

The Identity and Access Management (IAM) permission required for this operation is DeleteTable.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueDeleteConnection.deleteConnection
tools.description: Deletes a connection from the Data Catalog.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueDeleteCrawler.deleteCrawler
tools.description: Removes a specified crawler from the Glue Data Catalog, unless the crawler state is RUNNING.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueDeleteCustomEntityType.deleteCustomEntityType
tools.description: Deletes a custom pattern by specifying its name.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueDeleteDatabase.deleteDatabase
tools.description:
Removes a specified database from a Data Catalog.

After completing this operation, you no longer have access to the tables (and all table versions and partitions that might belong to the tables) and the user-defined functions in the deleted database. Glue deletes these "orphaned" resources asynchronously in a timely manner, at the discretion of the service.

To ensure the immediate deletion of all related resources, before calling DeleteDatabase, use DeleteTableVersion or BatchDeleteTableVersion, DeletePartition or BatchDeletePartition, DeleteUserDefinedFunction, and DeleteTable or BatchDeleteTable, to delete any resources that belong to the database.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueDeleteDataQualityRuleset.deleteDataQualityRuleset
tools.description: Deletes a data quality ruleset.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueDeleteDevEndpoint.deleteDevEndpoint
tools.description: Deletes a specified development endpoint.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueDeleteJob.deleteJob
tools.description: Deletes a specified job definition. If the job definition is not found, no exception is thrown.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueDeleteMlTransform.deleteMlTransform
tools.description: Deletes an Glue machine learning transform. Machine learning transforms are a special type of transform that use machine learning to learn the details of the transformation to be performed by learning from examples provided by humans. These transformations are then saved by Glue. If you no longer need a transform, you can delete it by calling DeleteMLTransforms. However, any Glue jobs that still reference the deleted transform will no longer succeed.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueDeletePartition.deletePartition
tools.description: Deletes a specified partition.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueDeletePartitionIndex.deletePartitionIndex
tools.description: Deletes a specified partition index from an existing table.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueDeleteRegistry.deleteRegistry
tools.description: Delete the entire registry including schema and all of its versions. To get the status of the delete operation, you can call the GetRegistry API after the asynchronous call. Deleting a registry will deactivate all online operations for the registry such as the UpdateRegistry, CreateSchema, UpdateSchema, and RegisterSchemaVersion APIs.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueDeleteResourcePolicy.deleteResourcePolicy
tools.description: Deletes a specified policy.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueDeleteSchema.deleteSchema
tools.description: Deletes the entire schema set, including the schema set and all of its versions. To get the status of the delete operation, you can call GetSchema API after the asynchronous call. Deleting a registry will deactivate all online operations for the schema, such as the GetSchemaByDefinition, and RegisterSchemaVersion APIs.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueDeleteSchemaVersions.deleteSchemaVersions
tools.description:
Remove versions from the specified schema. A version number or range may be supplied. If the compatibility mode forbids deleting of a version that is necessary, such as BACKWARDS_FULL, an error is returned. Calling the GetSchemaVersions API after this call will list the status of the deleted versions.

When the range of version numbers contain check pointed version, the API will return a 409 conflict and will not proceed with the deletion. You have to remove the checkpoint first using the DeleteSchemaCheckpoint API before using this API.

You cannot use the DeleteSchemaVersions API to delete the first schema version in the schema set. The first schema version can only be deleted by the DeleteSchema API. This operation will also delete the attached SchemaVersionMetadata under the schema versions. Hard deletes will be enforced on the database.

If the compatibility mode forbids deleting of a version that is necessary, such as BACKWARDS_FULL, an error is returned.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueDeleteSecurityConfiguration.deleteSecurityConfiguration
tools.description: Deletes a specified security configuration.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueDeleteSession.deleteSession
tools.description: Deletes the session.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueDeleteTable.deleteTable
tools.description:
Removes a table definition from the Data Catalog.

After completing this operation, you no longer have access to the table versions and partitions that belong to the deleted table. Glue deletes these "orphaned" resources asynchronously in a timely manner, at the discretion of the service.

To ensure the immediate deletion of all related resources, before calling DeleteTable, use DeleteTableVersion or BatchDeleteTableVersion, and DeletePartition or BatchDeletePartition, to delete any resources that belong to the table.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueDeleteTableVersion.deleteTableVersion
tools.description: Deletes a specified version of a table.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueDeleteTrigger.deleteTrigger
tools.description: Deletes a specified trigger. If the trigger is not found, no exception is thrown.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueDeleteUserDefinedFunction.deleteUserDefinedFunction
tools.description: Deletes an existing function definition from the Data Catalog.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueDeleteWorkflow.deleteWorkflow
tools.description: Deletes a workflow.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueGetBlueprint.getBlueprint
tools.description: Retrieves the details of a blueprint.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueGetBlueprintRun.getBlueprintRun
tools.description: Retrieves the details of a blueprint run.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueGetBlueprintRuns.getBlueprintRuns
tools.description: Retrieves the details of blueprint runs for a specified blueprint.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueGetCatalogImportStatus.getCatalogImportStatus
tools.description: Retrieves the status of a migration operation.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueGetClassifier.getClassifier
tools.description: Retrieve a classifier by name.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueGetClassifiers.getClassifiers
tools.description: Lists all classifier objects in the Data Catalog.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueGetColumnStatisticsForPartition.getColumnStatisticsForPartition
tools.description:
Retrieves partition statistics of columns.

The Identity and Access Management (IAM) permission required for this operation is GetPartition.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueGetColumnStatisticsForTable.getColumnStatisticsForTable
tools.description:
Retrieves table statistics of columns.

The Identity and Access Management (IAM) permission required for this operation is GetTable.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueGetConnection.getConnection
tools.description: Retrieves a connection definition from the Data Catalog.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueGetConnections.getConnections
tools.description: Retrieves a list of connection definitions from the Data Catalog.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueGetCrawler.getCrawler
tools.description: Retrieves metadata for a specified crawler.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueGetCrawlerMetrics.getCrawlerMetrics
tools.description: Retrieves metrics about specified crawlers.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueGetCrawlers.getCrawlers
tools.description: Retrieves metadata for all crawlers defined in the customer account.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueGetCustomEntityType.getCustomEntityType
tools.description: Retrieves the details of a custom pattern by specifying its name.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueGetDatabase.getDatabase
tools.description: Retrieves the definition of a specified database.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueGetDatabases.getDatabases
tools.description: Retrieves all databases defined in a given Data Catalog.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueGetDataCatalogEncryptionSettings.getDataCatalogEncryptionSettings
tools.description: Retrieves the security configuration for a specified catalog.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueGetDataflowGraph.getDataflowGraph
tools.description: Transforms a Python script into a directed acyclic graph (DAG).

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueGetDataQualityResult.getDataQualityResult
tools.description: Retrieves the result of a data quality rule evaluation.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueGetDataQualityRuleRecommendationRun.getDataQualityRuleRecommendationRun
tools.description: Gets the specified recommendation run that was used to generate rules.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueGetDataQualityRuleset.getDataQualityRuleset
tools.description: Returns an existing ruleset by identifier or name.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueGetDataQualityRulesetEvaluationRun.getDataQualityRulesetEvaluationRun
tools.description: Retrieves a specific run where a ruleset is evaluated against a data source.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueGetDevEndpoint.getDevEndpoint
tools.description:
Retrieves information about a specified development endpoint.

When you create a development endpoint in a virtual private cloud (VPC), Glue returns only a private IP address, and the public IP address field is not populated. When you create a non-VPC development endpoint, Glue returns only a public IP address.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueGetDevEndpoints.getDevEndpoints
tools.description:
Retrieves all the development endpoints in this Amazon Web Services account.

When you create a development endpoint in a virtual private cloud (VPC), Glue returns only a private IP address and the public IP address field is not populated. When you create a non-VPC development endpoint, Glue returns only a public IP address.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueGetJob.getJob
tools.description: Retrieves an existing job definition.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueGetJobBookmark.getJobBookmark
tools.description:
Returns information on a job bookmark entry.

For more information about enabling and using job bookmarks, see:

  • Tracking processed data using job bookmarks

  • Job parameters used by Glue

  • Job structure

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueGetJobRun.getJobRun
tools.description: Retrieves the metadata for a given job run.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueGetJobRuns.getJobRuns
tools.description: Retrieves metadata for all runs of a given job definition.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueGetJobs.getJobs
tools.description: Retrieves all current job definitions.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueGetMapping.getMapping
tools.description: Creates mappings.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueGetMlTaskRun.getMlTaskRun
tools.description: Gets details for a specific task run on a machine learning transform. Machine learning task runs are asynchronous tasks that Glue runs on your behalf as part of various machine learning workflows. You can check the stats of any task run by calling GetMLTaskRun with the TaskRunID and its parent transform's TransformID.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueGetMlTaskRuns.getMlTaskRuns
tools.description:
Gets a list of runs for a machine learning transform. Machine learning task runs are asynchronous tasks that Glue runs on your behalf as part of various machine learning workflows. You can get a sortable, filterable list of machine learning task runs by calling GetMLTaskRuns with their parent transform's TransformID and other optional parameters as documented in this section.

This operation returns a list of historic runs and must be paginated.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueGetMlTransform.getMlTransform
tools.description: Gets an Glue machine learning transform artifact and all its corresponding metadata. Machine learning transforms are a special type of transform that use machine learning to learn the details of the transformation to be performed by learning from examples provided by humans. These transformations are then saved by Glue. You can retrieve their metadata by calling GetMLTransform.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueGetMlTransforms.getMlTransforms
tools.description: Gets a sortable, filterable list of existing Glue machine learning transforms. Machine learning transforms are a special type of transform that use machine learning to learn the details of the transformation to be performed by learning from examples provided by humans. These transformations are then saved by Glue, and you can retrieve their metadata by calling GetMLTransforms.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueGetPartition.getPartition
tools.description: Retrieves information about a specified partition.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueGetPartitionIndexes.getPartitionIndexes
tools.description: Retrieves the partition indexes associated with a table.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueGetPartitions.getPartitions
tools.description: Retrieves information about the partitions in a table.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueGetPlan.getPlan
tools.description: Gets code to perform a specified mapping.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueGetRegistry.getRegistry
tools.description: Describes the specified registry in detail.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueGetResourcePolicies.getResourcePolicies
tools.description:
Retrieves the resource policies set on individual resources by Resource Access Manager during cross-account permission grants. Also retrieves the Data Catalog resource policy.

If you enabled metadata encryption in Data Catalog settings, and you do not have permission on the KMS key, the operation can't return the Data Catalog resource policy.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueGetResourcePolicy.getResourcePolicy
tools.description: Retrieves a specified resource policy.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueGetSchema.getSchema
tools.description: Describes the specified schema in detail.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueGetSchemaByDefinition.getSchemaByDefinition
tools.description: Retrieves a schema by the SchemaDefinition. The schema definition is sent to the Schema Registry, canonicalized, and hashed. If the hash is matched within the scope of the SchemaName or ARN (or the default registry, if none is supplied), that schema’s metadata is returned. Otherwise, a 404 or NotFound error is returned. Schema versions in Deleted statuses will not be included in the results.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueGetSchemaVersion.getSchemaVersion
tools.description: Get the specified schema by its unique ID assigned when a version of the schema is created or registered. Schema versions in Deleted status will not be included in the results.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueGetSchemaVersionsDiff.getSchemaVersionsDiff
tools.description:
Fetches the schema version difference in the specified difference type between two stored schema versions in the Schema Registry.

This API allows you to compare two schema versions between two schema definitions under the same schema.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueGetSecurityConfiguration.getSecurityConfiguration
tools.description: Retrieves a specified security configuration.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueGetSecurityConfigurations.getSecurityConfigurations
tools.description: Retrieves a list of all security configurations.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueGetSession.getSession
tools.description: Retrieves the session.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueGetStatement.getStatement
tools.description: Retrieves the statement.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueGetTable.getTable
tools.description: Retrieves the Table definition in a Data Catalog for a specified table.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueGetTables.getTables
tools.description: Retrieves the definitions of some or all of the tables in a given Database.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueGetTableVersion.getTableVersion
tools.description: Retrieves a specified version of a table.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueGetTableVersions.getTableVersions
tools.description: Retrieves a list of strings that identify available versions of a specified table.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueGetTags.getTags
tools.description: Retrieves a list of tags associated with a resource.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueGetTrigger.getTrigger
tools.description: Retrieves the definition of a trigger.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueGetTriggers.getTriggers
tools.description: Gets all the triggers associated with a job.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueGetUnfilteredPartitionMetadata.getUnfilteredPartitionMetadata
tools.description:
Retrieves partition metadata from the Data Catalog that contains unfiltered metadata.

For IAM authorization, the public IAM action associated with this API is glue:GetPartition.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueGetUnfilteredPartitionsMetadata.getUnfilteredPartitionsMetadata
tools.description:
Retrieves partition metadata from the Data Catalog that contains unfiltered metadata.

For IAM authorization, the public IAM action associated with this API is glue:GetPartitions.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueGetUnfilteredTableMetadata.getUnfilteredTableMetadata
tools.description:
Retrieves table metadata from the Data Catalog that contains unfiltered metadata.

For IAM authorization, the public IAM action associated with this API is glue:GetTable.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueGetUserDefinedFunction.getUserDefinedFunction
tools.description: Retrieves a specified function definition from the Data Catalog.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueGetUserDefinedFunctions.getUserDefinedFunctions
tools.description: Retrieves multiple function definitions from the Data Catalog.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueGetWorkflow.getWorkflow
tools.description: Retrieves resource metadata for a workflow.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueGetWorkflowRun.getWorkflowRun
tools.description: Retrieves the metadata for a given workflow run.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueGetWorkflowRunProperties.getWorkflowRunProperties
tools.description: Retrieves the workflow run properties which were set during the run.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueGetWorkflowRuns.getWorkflowRuns
tools.description: Retrieves metadata for all runs of a given workflow.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueImportCatalogToGlue.importCatalogToGlue
tools.description: Imports an existing Amazon Athena Data Catalog to Glue.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueListBlueprints.listBlueprints
tools.description: Lists all the blueprint names in an account.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueListCrawlers.listCrawlers
tools.description:
Retrieves the names of all crawler resources in this Amazon Web Services account, or the resources with the specified tag. This operation allows you to see which resources are available in your account, and their names.

This operation takes the optional Tags field, which you can use as a filter on the response so that tagged resources can be retrieved as a group. If you choose to use tags filtering, only resources with the tag are retrieved.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueListCrawls.listCrawls
tools.description:
Returns all the crawls of a specified crawler. Returns only the crawls that have occurred since the launch date of the crawler history feature, and only retains up to 12 months of crawls. Older crawls will not be returned.

You may use this API to:

  • Retrive all the crawls of a specified crawler.

  • Retrieve all the crawls of a specified crawler within a limited count.

  • Retrieve all the crawls of a specified crawler in a specific time range.

  • Retrieve all the crawls of a specified crawler with a particular state, crawl ID, or DPU hour value.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueListCustomEntityTypes.listCustomEntityTypes
tools.description: Lists all the custom patterns that have been created.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueListDataQualityResults.listDataQualityResults
tools.description: Returns all data quality execution results for your account.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueListDataQualityRuleRecommendationRuns.listDataQualityRuleRecommendationRuns
tools.description: Lists the recommendation runs meeting the filter criteria.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueListDataQualityRulesetEvaluationRuns.listDataQualityRulesetEvaluationRuns
tools.description: Lists all the runs meeting the filter criteria, where a ruleset is evaluated against a data source.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueListDataQualityRulesets.listDataQualityRulesets
tools.description: Returns a paginated list of rulesets for the specified list of Glue tables.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueListDevEndpoints.listDevEndpoints
tools.description:
Retrieves the names of all DevEndpoint resources in this Amazon Web Services account, or the resources with the specified tag. This operation allows you to see which resources are available in your account, and their names.

This operation takes the optional Tags field, which you can use as a filter on the response so that tagged resources can be retrieved as a group. If you choose to use tags filtering, only resources with the tag are retrieved.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueListJobs.listJobs
tools.description:
Retrieves the names of all job resources in this Amazon Web Services account, or the resources with the specified tag. This operation allows you to see which resources are available in your account, and their names.

This operation takes the optional Tags field, which you can use as a filter on the response so that tagged resources can be retrieved as a group. If you choose to use tags filtering, only resources with the tag are retrieved.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueListMlTransforms.listMlTransforms
tools.description: Retrieves a sortable, filterable list of existing Glue machine learning transforms in this Amazon Web Services account, or the resources with the specified tag. This operation takes the optional Tags field, which you can use as a filter of the responses so that tagged resources can be retrieved as a group. If you choose to use tag filtering, only resources with the tags are retrieved.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueListRegistries.listRegistries
tools.description: Returns a list of registries that you have created, with minimal registry information. Registries in the Deleting status will not be included in the results. Empty results will be returned if there are no registries available.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueListSchemas.listSchemas
tools.description:
Returns a list of schemas with minimal details. Schemas in Deleting status will not be included in the results. Empty results will be returned if there are no schemas available.

When the RegistryId is not provided, all the schemas across registries will be part of the API response.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueListSchemaVersions.listSchemaVersions
tools.description: Returns a list of schema versions that you have created, with minimal information. Schema versions in Deleted status will not be included in the results. Empty results will be returned if there are no schema versions available.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueListSessions.listSessions
tools.description: Retrieve a list of sessions.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueListStatements.listStatements
tools.description: Lists statements for the session.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueListTriggers.listTriggers
tools.description:
Retrieves the names of all trigger resources in this Amazon Web Services account, or the resources with the specified tag. This operation allows you to see which resources are available in your account, and their names.

This operation takes the optional Tags field, which you can use as a filter on the response so that tagged resources can be retrieved as a group. If you choose to use tags filtering, only resources with the tag are retrieved.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueListWorkflows.listWorkflows
tools.description: Lists names of workflows created in the account.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGluePutDataCatalogEncryptionSettings.putDataCatalogEncryptionSettings
tools.description: Sets the security configuration for a specified catalog. After the configuration has been set, the specified encryption is applied to every catalog write thereafter.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGluePutResourcePolicy.putResourcePolicy
tools.description: Sets the Data Catalog resource policy for access control.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGluePutSchemaVersionMetadata.putSchemaVersionMetadata
tools.description: Puts the metadata key value pair for a specified schema version ID. A maximum of 10 key value pairs will be allowed per schema version. They can be added over one or more calls.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGluePutWorkflowRunProperties.putWorkflowRunProperties
tools.description: Puts the specified workflow run properties for the given workflow run. If a property already exists for the specified run, then it overrides the value otherwise adds the property to existing properties.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueQuerySchemaVersionMetadata.querySchemaVersionMetadata
tools.description: Queries for the schema version metadata information.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueRegisterSchemaVersion.registerSchemaVersion
tools.description:
Adds a new version to the existing schema. Returns an error if new version of schema does not meet the compatibility requirements of the schema set. This API will not create a new schema set and will return a 404 error if the schema set is not already present in the Schema Registry.

If this is the first schema definition to be registered in the Schema Registry, this API will store the schema version and return immediately. Otherwise, this call has the potential to run longer than other operations due to compatibility modes. You can call the GetSchemaVersion API with the SchemaVersionId to check compatibility modes.

If the same schema definition is already stored in Schema Registry as a version, the schema ID of the existing schema is returned to the caller.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueRemoveSchemaVersionMetadata.removeSchemaVersionMetadata
tools.description: Removes a key value pair from the schema version metadata for the specified schema version ID.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueResetJobBookmark.resetJobBookmark
tools.description:
Resets a bookmark entry.

For more information about enabling and using job bookmarks, see:

  • Tracking processed data using job bookmarks

  • Job parameters used by Glue

  • Job structure

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueResumeWorkflowRun.resumeWorkflowRun
tools.description: Restarts selected nodes of a previous partially completed workflow run and resumes the workflow run. The selected nodes and all nodes that are downstream from the selected nodes are run.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueRunStatement.runStatement
tools.description: Executes the statement.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueSearchTables.searchTables
tools.description:
Searches a set of tables based on properties in the table metadata as well as on the parent database. You can search against text or filter conditions.

You can only get tables that you have access to based on the security policies defined in Lake Formation. You need at least a read-only access to the table for it to be returned. If you do not have access to all the columns in the table, these columns will not be searched against when returning the list of tables back to you. If you have access to the columns but not the data in the columns, those columns and the associated metadata for those columns will be included in the search.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueStartBlueprintRun.startBlueprintRun
tools.description: Starts a new run of the specified blueprint.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueStartCrawler.startCrawler
tools.description: Starts a crawl using the specified crawler, regardless of what is scheduled. If the crawler is already running, returns a CrawlerRunningException.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueStartCrawlerSchedule.startCrawlerSchedule
tools.description: Changes the schedule state of the specified crawler to SCHEDULED, unless the crawler is already running or the schedule state is already SCHEDULED.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueStartDataQualityRuleRecommendationRun.startDataQualityRuleRecommendationRun
tools.description: Starts a recommendation run that is used to generate rules when you don't know what rules to write. Glue Data Quality analyzes the data and comes up with recommendations for a potential ruleset. You can then triage the ruleset and modify the generated ruleset to your liking.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueStartDataQualityRulesetEvaluationRun.startDataQualityRulesetEvaluationRun
tools.description: Once you have a ruleset definition (either recommended or your own), you call this operation to evaluate the ruleset against a data source (Glue table). The evaluation computes results which you can retrieve with the GetDataQualityResult API.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueStartExportLabelsTaskRun.startExportLabelsTaskRun
tools.description: Begins an asynchronous task to export all labeled data for a particular transform. This task is the only label-related API call that is not part of the typical active learning workflow. You typically use StartExportLabelsTaskRun when you want to work with all of your existing labels at the same time, such as when you want to remove or change labels that were previously submitted as truth. This API operation accepts the TransformId whose labels you want to export and an Amazon Simple Storage Service (Amazon S3) path to export the labels to. The operation returns a TaskRunId. You can check on the status of your task run by calling the GetMLTaskRun API.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueStartImportLabelsTaskRun.startImportLabelsTaskRun
tools.description:
Enables you to provide additional labels (examples of truth) to be used to teach the machine learning transform and improve its quality. This API operation is generally used as part of the active learning workflow that starts with the StartMLLabelingSetGenerationTaskRun call and that ultimately results in improving the quality of your machine learning transform.

After the StartMLLabelingSetGenerationTaskRun finishes, Glue machine learning will have generated a series of questions for humans to answer. (Answering these questions is often called 'labeling' in the machine learning workflows). In the case of the FindMatches transform, these questions are of the form, “What is the correct way to group these rows together into groups composed entirely of matching records?” After the labeling process is finished, users upload their answers/labels with a call to StartImportLabelsTaskRun. After StartImportLabelsTaskRun finishes, all future runs of the machine learning transform use the new and improved labels and perform a higher-quality transformation.

By default, StartMLLabelingSetGenerationTaskRun continually learns from and combines all labels that you upload unless you set Replace to true. If you set Replace to true, StartImportLabelsTaskRun deletes and forgets all previously uploaded labels and learns only from the exact set that you upload. Replacing labels can be helpful if you realize that you previously uploaded incorrect labels, and you believe that they are having a negative effect on your transform quality.

You can check on the status of your task run by calling the GetMLTaskRun operation.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueStartJobRun.startJobRun
tools.description: Starts a job run using a job definition.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueStartMlEvaluationTaskRun.startMlEvaluationTaskRun
tools.description:
Starts a task to estimate the quality of the transform.

When you provide label sets as examples of truth, Glue machine learning uses some of those examples to learn from them. The rest of the labels are used as a test to estimate quality.

Returns a unique identifier for the run. You can call GetMLTaskRun to get more information about the stats of the EvaluationTaskRun.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueStartMlLabelingSetGenerationTaskRun.startMlLabelingSetGenerationTaskRun
tools.description:
Starts the active learning workflow for your machine learning transform to improve the transform's quality by generating label sets and adding labels.

When the StartMLLabelingSetGenerationTaskRun finishes, Glue will have generated a "labeling set" or a set of questions for humans to answer.

In the case of the FindMatches transform, these questions are of the form, “What is the correct way to group these rows together into groups composed entirely of matching records?”

After the labeling process is finished, you can upload your labels with a call to StartImportLabelsTaskRun. After StartImportLabelsTaskRun finishes, all future runs of the machine learning transform will use the new and improved labels and perform a higher-quality transformation.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueStartTrigger.startTrigger
tools.description: Starts an existing trigger. See Triggering Jobs for information about how different types of trigger are started.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueStartWorkflowRun.startWorkflowRun
tools.description: Starts a new run of the specified workflow.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueStopCrawler.stopCrawler
tools.description: If the specified crawler is running, stops the crawl.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueStopCrawlerSchedule.stopCrawlerSchedule
tools.description: Sets the schedule state of the specified crawler to NOT_SCHEDULED, but does not stop the crawler if it is already running.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueStopSession.stopSession
tools.description: Stops the session.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueStopTrigger.stopTrigger
tools.description: Stops a specified trigger.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueStopWorkflowRun.stopWorkflowRun
tools.description: Stops the execution of the specified workflow run.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueTagResource.tagResource
tools.description: Adds tags to a resource. A tag is a label you can assign to an Amazon Web Services resource. In Glue, you can tag only certain resources. For information about what resources you can tag, see Amazon Web Services Tags in Glue.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueUntagResource.untagResource
tools.description: Removes tags from a resource.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueUpdateBlueprint.updateBlueprint
tools.description: Updates a registered blueprint.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueUpdateClassifier.updateClassifier
tools.description: Modifies an existing classifier (a GrokClassifier, an XMLClassifier, a JsonClassifier, or a CsvClassifier, depending on which field is present).

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueUpdateColumnStatisticsForPartition.updateColumnStatisticsForPartition
tools.description:
Creates or updates partition statistics of columns.

The Identity and Access Management (IAM) permission required for this operation is UpdatePartition.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueUpdateColumnStatisticsForTable.updateColumnStatisticsForTable
tools.description:
Creates or updates table statistics of columns.

The Identity and Access Management (IAM) permission required for this operation is UpdateTable.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueUpdateConnection.updateConnection
tools.description: Updates a connection definition in the Data Catalog.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueUpdateCrawler.updateCrawler
tools.description: Updates a crawler. If a crawler is running, you must stop it using StopCrawler before updating it.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueUpdateCrawlerSchedule.updateCrawlerSchedule
tools.description: Updates the schedule of a crawler using a cron expression.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueUpdateDatabase.updateDatabase
tools.description: Updates an existing database definition in a Data Catalog.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueUpdateDataQualityRuleset.updateDataQualityRuleset
tools.description: Updates the specified data quality ruleset.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueUpdateDevEndpoint.updateDevEndpoint
tools.description: Updates a specified development endpoint.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueUpdateJob.updateJob
tools.description: Updates an existing job definition. The previous job definition is completely overwritten by this information.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueUpdateJobFromSourceControl.updateJobFromSourceControl
tools.description:
Synchronizes a job from the source control repository. This operation takes the job artifacts that are located in the remote repository and updates the Glue internal stores with these artifacts.

This API supports optional parameters which take in the repository information.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueUpdateMlTransform.updateMlTransform
tools.description:
Updates an existing machine learning transform. Call this operation to tune the algorithm parameters to achieve better results.

After calling this operation, you can call the StartMLEvaluationTaskRun operation to assess how well your new parameters achieved your goals (such as improving the quality of your machine learning transform, or making it more cost-effective).

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueUpdatePartition.updatePartition
tools.description: Updates a partition.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueUpdateRegistry.updateRegistry
tools.description: Updates an existing registry which is used to hold a collection of schemas. The updated properties relate to the registry, and do not modify any of the schemas within the registry.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueUpdateSchema.updateSchema
tools.description:
Updates the description, compatibility setting, or version checkpoint for a schema set.

For updating the compatibility setting, the call will not validate compatibility for the entire set of schema versions with the new compatibility setting. If the value for Compatibility is provided, the VersionNumber (a checkpoint) is also required. The API will validate the checkpoint version number for consistency.

If the value for the VersionNumber (checkpoint) is provided, Compatibility is optional and this can be used to set/reset a checkpoint for the schema.

This update will happen only if the schema is in the AVAILABLE state.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueUpdateSourceControlFromJob.updateSourceControlFromJob
tools.description:
Synchronizes a job to the source control repository. This operation takes the job artifacts from the Glue internal stores and makes a commit to the remote repository that is configured on the job.

This API supports optional parameters which take in the repository information.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueUpdateTable.updateTable
tools.description: Updates a metadata table in the Data Catalog.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueUpdateTrigger.updateTrigger
tools.description: Updates a trigger definition.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueUpdateUserDefinedFunction.updateUserDefinedFunction
tools.description: Updates an existing function definition in the Data Catalog.

tools.name: xAmzTargetAwsGlueUpdateWorkflow.updateWorkflow
tools.description: Updates an existing workflow.

tools.name: openapi.previewSpec
tools.description: Preview an OpenAPI document before adding it as a source

tools.name: openapi.addSource
tools.description: Add an OpenAPI source and register its operations as tools
